title: Kafka Streams Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on KStreams, KTables, state stores, and stream processing concepts.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a KStream and a KTable?

options.text: A KStream handles inserts only, while a KTable behaves like a changelog where new records overwrite old ones with the same key.
A KStream is stored in memory, while a KTable is strictly stored on disk.
A KStream can be joined to other topics, but a KTable cannot.
A KTable is for batch processing and KStream is for real-time processing.

questions.question: What local database does Kafka Streams embed by default to manage stateful aggregations?

options.text: SQLite
Redis
RocksDB
Cassandra

questions.question: Which infrastructure requirement differentiates Kafka Streams from Apache Spark or Flink?

options.text: Kafka Streams requires a Hadoop YARN cluster to run.
Kafka Streams does NOT require a separate processing cluster; it runs as a standard application library.
Kafka Streams requires Zookeeper to manage its streaming tasks.
Kafka Streams requires an Apache Airflow scheduler.

questions.question: If you need to join a very small, rarely changing dimension topic (like Zip Codes) cleanly to a massive stream, what abstraction is best?

options.text: KStream
KTable
GlobalKTable
Change Data Stream

questions.question: What does it mean for two Kafka topics to be 'co-partitioned' before a standard join?

options.text: They must have the exact same number of partitions and the same partitioning strategy.
They must be hosted on the same Kafka broker.
They must use the exact same AVRO schema.
They must have the same offset retention policy.
